zh.Llanuwchllyn, Merionethshire, Wales: 英國威爾斯梅利奧爾利安可利
zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Verch Broughton於1380年出生在英國威爾斯梅利奧爾利安可利。她於1398年與Sir Griffith Vaughan在英國威爾斯梅利奧爾利安可利結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1409年在英國威爾斯享年29歲。

nameMeanings.name: Broughton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the many places in all parts of England called Broughton. The first element is variously Old English brōc ‘brook’, burg ‘fortress’, or beorg ‘hill, mound’; the second is in all cases Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Braughton
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Broughton  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: Verch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Orlo.

variants.name: Ferch
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Verch Broughton was born in 1380, in Llanuwchllyn, Merionethshire, Wales, United Kingdom. She married Sir Griffith Vaughan in 1398, in Llanuwchllyn, Merionethshire, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died in 1409, in Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 29.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Verch Broughton est née en 1380, à Llanuwchllyn, Merionethshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Sir Griffith Vaughan en 1398, à Llanuwchllyn, Merionethshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1409, à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 29 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Verch Broughton(은)는 1380년 영국 웨일스 머이오네스실르 랜w클린에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1398년 영국 웨일스 머이오네스실르 랜w클린에서 Sir Griffith Vaughan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1409년 영국 웨일스에서 29세의 나이로 사망했다.
